task: In Simple Gallery Pro, copy receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg in DCIM and save a copy with the same name in Download

steps.thought:
[Observation]
The current screen is the phone's home screen. Visible apps include Gmail, Photos, YouTube, Chrome, Phone, Messages, and a Google search widget. The target app, "Simple Gallery Pro", is not visible on this screen.

[Plan]
Step 1: Open Simple Gallery Pro app → | Step 2: Navigate to the DCIM folder | Step 3: Select "receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg" | Step 4: Copy the file and paste it into the Download folder

[Decision]
Since the task requires performing file operations within "Simple Gallery Pro" and the app is not visible on the current home screen page, I need to launch it. I will use the system's open app function to launch "Simple Gallery Pro" directly.

[Memory]
First action, no prior memory.

[Observation]
The current screen displays the main interface of Simple Gallery Pro. There are two visible folders: "DCIM" on the left, showing a thumbnail of an expense receipt, and another folder on the right containing text like "Expense: Furnishing" and "Expense: Public Transit". The search bar is at the top.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple Gallery Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the DCIM folder → | Step 3: Select "receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg" | Step 4: Copy the file and paste it into the Download folder

[Decision]
Based on the current plan, the next step is to navigate into the DCIM folder where the target file is located. I can see the "DCIM" folder on the left side of the screen. I will tap on it to open it and view its contents.

[Memory]
Target file: receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg. Target destination: Download folder. Current location: App main screen.

[Plan Update]
Step 1: Open Simple Gallery Pro app ✓ | Step 2: Navigate to the DCIM folder ✓ | Step 3: Select "receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg" → | Step 4: Copy the file and paste it into the Download folder

[Decision]
Based on the current plan, I need to select the target file "receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg" to initiate the copy process. In gallery apps, selecting an item to perform actions like copy usually requires a long press on the item itself rather than a simple tap which would just open it. Therefore, I will long press the image file to select it.

[Memory]
Target file: receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg (located in DCIM). Target destination: Download folder. Current location: DCIM folder view.

[Observation]
The screen displays the image "receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg" in full view. The file is selected, indicated by a blue checkmark on the image thumbnail. The top action bar has changed to show options for selected items: a trash bin icon, a share icon, and a three-dot menu icon (More options). The text "1 / 1" is displayed at the top left, confirming one item is selected.

[Plan Update]
Step 1-3: Open app, navigate to DCIM, select target file ✓ | Step 4: Open menu to copy the file → | Step 5: Select destination "Download" folder and paste

[Decision]
Based on the current plan, I need to initiate the copy process. The file is already selected. The copy option is not directly visible on the top bar (only delete and share are visible). Therefore, I need to access the extended options menu where the "Copy" action should be located. I will tap the three-dot menu icon in the top right corner to find the copy function.

[Memory]
Target file: receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg (selected). Target destination: Download folder. Current location: Image view with selection active.

The screen shows a dropdown menu opened over the image selection view. The menu contains several options including "Rotate", "Properties", "Rename", "Hide", "Copy to", "Move to", "Create shortcut", "Open with", "Set as", "Resize", "Edit", "Add to favorites", "Fix Date Taken value", and "Select all". The image "receipt_copy_proud_monkey.jpg" remains selected in the background.

Step 1-4: Select target file and open menu ✓ | Step 5: Select "Copy to" option → | Step 6: Select destination "Download" folder and confirm paste

[Decision]
Based on the current plan, the next step is to initiate the copy process. The dropdown menu is now visible, and the "Copy to" option is clearly listed among the choices. I will tap on "Copy to" to proceed with copying the selected image to the target folder.

The "DCIM" folder is currently selected or visible at the top, but the target destination "Download" is not in the immediate list. There is an "Other folder" button at the bottom left of the dialog.

To find it, I need to open the broader folder selection interface. Therefore, I will tap the "Other folder" button at the bottom left to navigate to the system's file manager or browse all available folders to locate "Download".
